task: Editor Portal v2 — Auto-tickets, First-Claim Locking, Star Ratings, Asset Access
description: These are ADDITIONS and CHANGES on top of the already-working editor portal (v1). Do not re-implement what is already there. Read each file before editing. The existing system is healthy — make surgical changes only.
context_of_what_already_works: GET /api/editor/tickets — lists tickets assigned to current editor
GET /api/editor/badge — open/in_progress/review counts for current editor
Ticket, TicketNote models exist in backend/app/models/__init__.py
what_changes_in_v2: 1. Auto-ticket creation: when a project is processed in studio.py, create a Ticket automatically (no manual Admin step needed)
2. Claim system: tickets start unassigned. Any editor sees the pool. First editor to call /claim gets exclusive ownership. Others cannot access it after.
3. Editor list view: show BOTH unassigned (claimable) tickets AND editor's own claimed tickets in separate tabs
4. Star rating: Admin rates each editor per completed ticket (1–5 stars). Ratings are stored and averaged.
5. Editor access to owner assets: ticket response exposes the original photo URL and the owner's logo endpoint
6. Badge update: add available_count (unassigned tickets waiting for any editor)

phase_A_auto_ticket_on_processing.title: Phase A — Auto-create Ticket after Image Processing
phase_A_auto_ticket_on_processing.description: When studio.py finishes processing an image and saves a Project record, immediately create a Ticket for it. No Admin action required.

step_A1_save_original_to_static.where: After line `original_image.save(gen_dir / 'original.png', format='PNG')` (around line 387), ADD a second save:
code_to_add: # Also save original to static/originals/ so editors can access it via HTTP
logger.warning('Failed to save original to static (non-fatal): %s', e)
step_A1_save_original_to_static.note: Define ORIGINALS_DIR at module level alongside RESULTS_DIR (around line 24). Move `original_static_url` to module-scope variable so it's accessible later in the function.

step_A2_auto_create_ticket.where: Inside the `if current_user and db and result_url:` block, AFTER `project_id = proj.id` (around line 476), add:
code_to_add: # Auto-create editor ticket for this project
from app.models import Ticket as TicketModel
assigned_to_id=None,  # Unassigned — first editor to claim gets it
original_image_url=original_static_url,  # Raw photo before AI processing
logger.info('Auto-created ticket %s for project %s', auto_ticket.id, proj.id)
step_A2_auto_create_ticket.important: The auto-ticket has assigned_to_id=NULL. This is the signal that it is claimable by any editor. Do NOT set created_by_id — leave NULL to distinguish auto-created tickets from admin-created ones.

step_A3_add_project_image_url_to_ticket.what: We need the AI-processed result URL accessible in the ticket so editors can see what the AI produced alongside the raw original.
step_A3_add_project_image_url_to_ticket.change: Add column `ai_result_url = Column(Text, nullable=True)` to the Ticket model. This stores the studio.py result URL (the composited image).
step_A3_add_project_image_url_to_ticket.migration: Add to init_db(): `_add_col_if_missing(conn, 'tickets', 'ai_result_url', 'TEXT', ticket_cols)` where ticket_cols = [c['name'] for c in inspector.get_columns('tickets')]
step_A3_add_project_image_url_to_ticket.also_add_to_ALLOWED_COLS: Add 'ai_result_url' to the tickets entry in _ALLOWED_COLS
step_A3_add_project_image_url_to_ticket.set_in_studio_py: In the auto_ticket creation block, also set: `auto_ticket.ai_result_url = result_url`

phase_B_claim_system.title: Phase B — First-Come-First-Served Claim System
phase_B_claim_system.description: Any EDITOR can see unassigned tickets. The first one to call /claim gets exclusive ownership. After claiming, no other editor can see or access that ticket.

step_B1_update_list_endpoint.current_behavior: Shows only tickets where assigned_to_id == current_user.id
step_B1_update_list_endpoint.new_behavior: Shows: (1) tickets assigned to current_user, AND (2) unassigned tickets (assigned_to_id IS NULL) that are not yet done/rejected. Admins still see all.

code_change.replace_this:
if current_user.role == Role.EDITOR:
        q = q.filter(Ticket.assigned_to_id == current_user.id)
with_this: # Exclude tickets that are done or rejected and unassigned (cleanup)

step_B2_add_claim_endpoint.description: Atomically claim an unassigned ticket. Uses a SQL-level lock to prevent two editors claiming simultaneously.
implementation: # Atomic claim: UPDATE tickets SET assigned_to_id=X WHERE id=Y AND assigned_to_id IS NULL
# rowcount == 0 means someone else claimed it first
' WHERE id=:tid AND assigned_to_id IS NULL RETURNING id'
# Check if ticket exists at all
# Ticket exists but is already claimed by someone else
raise HTTPException(status_code=409, detail='Ticket already claimed by another editor')
# Send assignment email to the editor (non-blocking)
step_B2_add_claim_endpoint.note: The RETURNING clause works on PostgreSQL. The atomic UPDATE ensures race-condition safety — two editors cannot claim the same ticket even under concurrent requests.

step_B3_update_require_ticket_helper.note: The current helper is correct — it already blocks editors from accessing other editors' claimed tickets. No change needed here.

step_B4_update_badge_endpoint.change: Add available_count (unassigned, claimable tickets) to the response

schema_change.add_field: available_count: int = 0  — count of unassigned open tickets any editor can claim

phase_C_star_ratings.title: Phase C — Editor Star Rating System (1–5)
phase_C_star_ratings.description: Admin rates each editor after a ticket is marked Done. Ratings are stored per-ticket and averaged on the editor's profile. All editors can see their own average. Admins see averages in the editors list.

columns.ticket_id: Integer, ForeignKey('tickets.id', ondelete='CASCADE'), nullable=False, unique=True — one rating per ticket
columns.note: Text, nullable=True — optional comment from Admin

add_to_User.rating_avg: Column(Float, default=0.0) — denormalized average, updated on each new rating
add_to_User.rating_count: Column(Integer, default=0) — total number of ratings received

migration_in_init_db: # EditorRating table is created automatically by Base.metadata.create_all()

add_fields.owner_logo_url: Optional[str] = None — URL to fetch the project owner's logo (editor can download it)
add_fields.ai_result_url: Optional[str] = None — the AI-composited result from studio processing

step_C3_rate_endpoint.description: Admin rates a completed ticket. One rating per ticket — calling again updates the existing rating.
implementation: raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail='Cannot rate an unassigned ticket')
# Recompute denormalized avg on the editor User record
from sqlalchemy import func as sqlfunc

step_C4_expose_rating_in_editor_response.change: After building the TicketResponse, populate the rating field if ticket.rating exists

step_C5_update_editors_list.change: Populate rating_avg, rating_count, and completed_ticket_count in EditorUserResponse
code: from app.models import Ticket as TicketModel

phase_D_editor_asset_access.title: Phase D — Editor Access to Owner Assets (Logo + Original Photos)
phase_D_editor_asset_access.description: When an editor claims a ticket, they need the original car photo AND the project owner's logo (if any) to do quality retouching. These are provided via secure endpoints that only the assigned editor can access.

step_D1_logo_endpoint.description: Returns the project owner's logo image as a file download. Only the assigned editor (or Admin) can call this.
implementation: ticket = _require_ticket(db, ticket_id, current_user)  # enforces ownership check
if not owner or not owner.logo_data:
raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail='Owner has no logo')

step_D2_ticket_response_owner_logo_url.change: Populate owner_logo_url and ai_result_url in TicketResponse
code: # Determine if owner has a logo
if ticket.project and ticket.project.owner and ticket.project.owner.logo_data:
# ai_result_url comes from ticket.ai_result_url (set during auto-ticket creation)
step_D2_ticket_response_owner_logo_url.note: This tells the editor 'there is a logo available at this URL' — they can download it to use as reference or reapply it in their retouching.

step_D3_ensure_owner_eager_loaded.change: Add joinedload for project.owner to avoid N+1 queries
code: # In list_tickets, add the same .options() chain to the query `q`

phase_E_frontend_changes.title: Phase E — Frontend UI Changes for v2
phase_E_frontend_changes.description: Update the editor portal frontend to reflect: claim button on unassigned tickets, star rating display, two-tab list view.

step_E1_ticket_list_tabs.change: Change filter tabs to: Available (unassigned, status=open) | My Open | In Progress | In Review | Done
step_E1_ticket_list_tabs.available_tab_query: Pass ?claimed=false to GET /api/editor/tickets or filter client-side: items where assigned_to_id is null
step_E1_ticket_list_tabs.note: The backend already returns both unassigned and editor's own tickets. Client-side: check if item.assigned_to_id is null to determine if it's claimable.

step_E2_claim_button.change: On ticket cards where assigned_to_id is null: show a 'Claim' button instead of a detail link. Clicking calls POST /api/editor/tickets/{id}/claim. On success: navigate to /editor/tickets/{id}. On 409: show toast 'Already claimed by another editor'.
step_E2_claim_button.button_appearance: Primary button with a padlock-open icon. Label: t('editor.tickets.claim')

step_E3_ticket_detail_asset_section.change: Add a 'Project Assets' section showing: (1) Original Photo download button (ticket.original_image_url), (2) AI Result preview (ticket.ai_result_url), (3) Owner Logo download button (only show if ticket.owner_logo_url is not null)
step_E3_ticket_detail_asset_section.note: All three are download links — the editor downloads them, retouches in their tool of choice, then uploads the result.

step_E4_rating_display_on_editor_me_page.change: Show star rating: display rating_avg as filled stars (1–5) + rating_count below. Example: '★★★★☆ (12 ratings)'. Use the GET /api/editor/me endpoint which now includes rating_avg and rating_count.

step_E5_admin_rate_button.change: When ticket.status == 'done' and ticket.assigned_to_id is not null: show a 'Rate Editor' section with 5 star buttons (1–5) and an optional note textarea. Submit calls POST /admin/editor/tickets/{id}/rate. Show current rating if ticket.rating exists.

step_E6_editors_list_ratings.change: Add Rating column to the editors table: show star average (e.g. '4.2 ★') and count in parens, and completed ticket count. Sort by rating descending by default.

step_E7_badge_available_dot.change: Update badge polling to use the new available_count field from GET /api/editor/badge. Show two badges on the nav: (1) red dot with count for editor's own pending tickets (open + in_progress + review), (2) a separate green dot or number for available_count (claimable tickets waiting). This tells editors at a glance 'there are 3 projects available to claim'.

phase_F_i18n_additions.title: Phase F — New i18n Strings for v2
phase_F_i18n_additions.what: Add these keys inside the existing 'editor' object — do NOT duplicate or remove existing keys

add_under_editor_tickets_sv.alreadyClaimed: Redan taget av en annan redaktör

phase_H_api_client_additions.title: Phase H — New API Client Functions
add_functions: adminRateTicket(ticketId: number, stars: number, note?: string): Promise<EditorRating>  → POST /admin/editor/tickets/{id}/rate

implementation_order: Phase A (auto-ticket) — test by processing one image and checking tickets table in DB
Phase C steps C1 (model + migration) — restart backend, verify editor_ratings table created
Phase C steps C2–C5 (schemas + rate endpoint + list update)
Phase B steps B1–B4 (claim system) — test with two concurrent requests to verify atomicity
Rebuild: docker compose down && docker compose build --no-cache backend frontend && docker compose up -d
verification_steps: Process one image via the app → check DB: SELECT * FROM tickets ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1; — should have assigned_to_id=NULL, status='open', original_image_url set
Login as editor → GET /api/editor/tickets → should include the unassigned ticket in response
POST /api/editor/tickets/{id}/claim again (same ticket, different editor session) → should return 409
Login as second editor → GET /api/editor/tickets → claimed ticket should NOT appear
Mark ticket done → POST /admin/editor/tickets/{id}/rate with stars=4 → verify editor_ratings row inserted, user.rating_avg updated
GET /api/admin/editor/editors → should show updated rating_avg and completed_ticket_count
GET /api/editor/tickets/{id}/owner-logo for ticket with owner who has logo → returns image bytes
critical_notes: The RETURNING clause in the claim UPDATE (Phase B2) requires PostgreSQL — already confirmed as the DB engine.
Do NOT change the existing POST /api/admin/editor/tickets endpoint — Admin can still create tickets manually for edge cases. Auto-tickets and manual tickets coexist.
original_image_url on the ticket = the raw photo before AI. ai_result_url = the AI-composited image. These are different files and both are useful to the editor.
The original_static_url variable in studio.py must be initialized to None BEFORE the try/except block that sets it, and referenced AFTER — verify scope is correct.
EditorRating uses unique=True on ticket_id — only one rating per ticket is enforced at DB level. The upsert logic in the rate endpoint handles the case where Admin updates their rating.
Logo data (user.logo_data) is stored as BYTEA — never return it in JSON. Always serve through the /owner-logo streaming endpoint.
